checks.a_analysis: The output accurately reflects the content found in the source document. The titles for the sections ('Slide Number 1', 'Slide Number 2', 'Slide Number 3') are structural additions allowed by the auditing guidelines. All text content, such as titles, dates, and sources, corresponds to the text visible in the source images and OCR.

The contact emails provided on the final slide (katie.doherty@rutherfordcountync.gov, relkins@sosnc.gov, and elizabeth.daniel@nc.gov) were verified against the source document. They match the source exactly without any fabrications or errors.
All three slides present in the source document are represented in the output JSON. Each slide is contained within its own section with its corresponding text and image elements.
There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant text content is missing from the output. All text from the title slide, the status map slide (including the sources text), and the concluding contact slide is present.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output is highly faithful to the source. Minor differences in whitespace or line breaks are considered acceptable reformatting. The meaning and data points (such as dates) are preserved accurately.

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully reviewed the source document and the output JSON. The content in the output follows the source page by page. The 'NC DIT' logo appears on every page of the source PDF, and accordingly, it is included in each section of the output JSON. There are no instances of text blocks or elements being duplicated in the output that only appear once in the source.

The output uses Level 2 headings for the titles of each slide (e.g., 'NC Parcels', 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', and 'Thank you!'). While there is no Level 1 heading, the hierarchy is consistent and does not skip levels (e.g., jumping from H2 to H4). This is considered an acceptable normalization for accessibility purposes.
The source document does not contain any tables. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

The reading order in the output JSON faithfully follows the visual flow of the source PDF. For instance, on Page 2, the title and date are followed by the map, then the source information, and finally the decorative logos. This matches the logical progression intended by the original layout.

The source document contains text under 'Sources' on Page 2 that is formatted as multiple lines, resembling a list. The output renders this as a single paragraph with line breaks, which correctly preserves the structural meaning and information flow.

I have verified the links in the output against the source. Page 3 of the source contains three email addresses. The output correctly identifies these and wraps them in 'mailto' links. No broken, missing, or fabricated links were found.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the sequential organization of the source PDF, presenting the content slide-by-slide. While there are expected changes such as the addition of a Table of Contents for navigation and shifts in element positioning (e.g., the NCDIT logo on page 1 moved from a sidebar to a centered position), the overall visual hierarchy and flow of information remain consistent with the source document.
The source document does not contain any tables to evaluate.

All meaningful images and logos from the source PDF are present in the HTML version. This includes the NCDIT logos on pages 1 and 3, and the project status map (which contains the NCGICC logo) on page 2.

Significant body content is visually duplicated in 'Slide Number 2'. The conversion has extracted the text from PDF page 2 (Title, Date, Legend items, and Sources) and rendered it as HTML text. However, it also renders a full-page image of that same PDF page immediately below the extracted text. This results in the slide title, date, legend, and source attribution appearing twice within the main body of the slide—once as text and once within the image block.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 of the PDF contains the title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', date 'April 9, 2025', legend text, and sources text.
checks.b_evidence_output: Slide Number 2 in the HTML displays the title, date, legend, and sources as text, followed by an image of the original page that duplicates all of this information.
